heading: Authorization for use of Coast Guard Academy facilities and equipment by covered foundations
label: 1908 Authorization for use of Coast Guard Academy facilities and equipment by covered foundations
notes: References in TextSection 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, referred to in subsec. (f)(1), is classified to , Internal Revenue Code.section 501(c)(3) of Title 26

children.heading: .—Authority
children.label: a .—Authority
children.text: Subject to subsections (b) and (c), the Secretary, with the concurrence of the Superintendent of the Coast Guard Academy, may authorize a covered foundation to use, on a reimbursable or nonreimbursable basis as determined by the Secretary, facilities or equipment of the Coast Guard Academy.

children.heading: .—Prohibition
children.label: b .—Prohibition
children.text: The Secretary may not authorize any use of facilities or equipment under subsection (a) if such use may jeopardize the health, safety, or well-being of any member of the Coast Guard or cadet of the Coast Guard Academy.
is without any liability of the United States to the covered foundation;

children.label: A
children.text: affect the ability of any official or employee of the Coast Guard, or any member of the armed forces, to carry out any responsibility or duty in a fair and objective manner;

children.label: B
children.text: compromise the integrity or appearance of integrity of any program of the Coast Guard, or any individual involved in any such program; or

children.label: C
children.text: include the participation of any cadet of the Coast Guard Academy at an event of the covered foundation, other than participation of such a cadet in an honor guard;
complies with any applicable ethics regulation; and
has been reviewed and approved by an attorney of the Coast Guard.

children.heading: .—Issuance of Policies
children.label: d .—Issuance of Policies
children.text: The Secretary shall issue Coast Guard policies to carry out this section.

children.heading: .—Briefing
children.label: e .—Briefing
children.text: For any fiscal year in which the Secretary exercises the authority under subsection (a), not later than the last day of such fiscal year, the Commandant shall provide a briefing to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives on the number of events or activities of a covered foundation supported by such exercise of authority during the fiscal year.
is a charitable, educational, or civic nonprofit organization under section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986; and

children.label: A
children.text: recruiting activities with respect to the Coast Guard Academy;

children.label: B
children.text: parent or alumni development in support of the Coast Guard Academy;

children.label: C
children.text: academic, leadership, or character development of Coast Guard Academy cadets;

children.label: D
children.text: institutional development of the Coast Guard Academy; or

children.label: E
children.text: athletics in support of the Coast Guard Academy.
